notes: Disable D3d11 with  PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Cursor flickers in main menu. Singleplayer works perfect, albeit a few crashes during a several-hour campaign. Haven't tested multiplayer but PunkBuster looks like it's working fine in the background.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Using the "PROTON_NO_D3D11=1 %command%" command on launch options will help run the game. It has a problem with the mouse during the boat scene at the start, but it doesn't really get in the way as everything works when the cutscene is over. You'll have to manually register on EA's website in order to play multiplayer -- it won't register you in-game somehow.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: So the game installed without any problems including the Punkbuster Anti Cheat Service. The DLCs worked as well. Everything is playable, I played hours of Singleplayer and Multiplayer, the only issue is the performance. When either starting Singleplayer or joining a Multiplayer match the game lags tremendously but after playing for a little while it'll get better. But yeah the performance was quite a bit better and stable on Windows. It may improve in the near future as Valve continues to work on Proton.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Runs fine in single player. MP not tested. Mouse pointer glitches out in menus, but kinda working. Overall good.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: This game is a Oldie but a goldie for me. Sure its getting old and dated now but its a great game and I come back to it always. Just something about it. No issues to report on at all. It runs reliably just like on windows.

notes: Works perfectly from my observation with 3.7-8 with the recommended tweaks (opengl instead of Vulkan and without d3d11). Works mostly well with stuttering with the same tweaks on Proton 4.2-6. Tested on multiple maps in multiplayer.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Works perfectly out of the box
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes:
Works very well as-is, approx. a few missions into the campaign.
Cutscenes may stutter a bit, and the camera is more unsteady than I remember it was on PS3.
Occasional frame drops, but nothing serious.
Wine D3D11 and/or no D3D11 tweaks made frame rate much worse.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Runs as good as the native version out of the box, but you need to give it a few minutes to compile all the shaders (it will be laggy until then). You have to enter your login credentials every time you start the game (and you have to type them manually because the login form doesn't support copying and pasting). If you check "Remember password" or "Auto login" it will throw the connection error. Also, the game froze one time, when I clicked "Exit" after a battle. I had to kill the process.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: Game runs fine for me on the highest settings. Sometimes there is stutter and frame drops but seems rare. Multiplayer works fine, even on Punkbuster servers, however, in the beginning of a multiplayer match there is some stutter and FPS issues but after a little it stops and the game is generally playable.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2

notes: with AZERTY, you'll need to reset the "2"nd weapon keybind each time. Runs @200fps maxed out, needs caching the level on the first run.
title: Battlefield: Bad Company™ 2
